key_insight: FormalAnalyticGeo turns free-form analytic geometry problems into verifiable multimodal data by using a formal intermediate language and automated diagram rendering/answer extraction.
engineering_relevance: It provides a scalable, annotation-free pipeline for building high-quality training datasets for multimodal math reasoning and geometry-focused AI systems.
method: Generate analytic geometry problems with an LLM.
Convert each problem into CDL, then render precise diagrams with an SDF engine.
Use vision-based measurement plus quality verification and retry loops to obtain ground-truth answers and filter errors.
evidence_signal: The resulting AnalyticGeo7K dataset contains over 7K verified multimodal problems, with a median ground-truth relative error of 0.70% and 82.3% of answers within 5% of the exact symbolic solution.
watch_item: The pipeline depends on accurate formalization and rendering; any CDL conversion or SDF measurement drift could propagate subtle geometric errors into the dataset.
paper_title: FormalAnalyticGeo: A Neural-Symbolic Based Framework for Multimodal Analytic Geometry Problem Generation
